title: The Word That Heals from Afar
subtitle: Christ heals the official's dying son at Cana, with a single word

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
The Lord is present everywhere, as God.
Wait for him in quiet, as one who is sick waits for healing.
gospel: When Christ came again into Cana of Galilee,
where in the wedding he had made water into wine,
a certain official came to him,
whose son lay sick at Capernaum.
He begged him to come down and heal his son,
for the boy was beginning to die.
Jesus said: Unless you see signs and wonders, you do not believe.
The official said: Lord, come down before my son dies.
Jesus said to him: Go; your son lives.
The man believed the word that Jesus spoke, and went.
His servants met him and told him: Your son lives.
He asked them the hour in which he grew better.
They said: Yesterday, at the seventh hour, the fever left him.
The father knew it was the very hour in which Jesus had said: Your son lives.
And he himself believed, and his whole household.
behold: There was a certain official, whose son lay sick at Capernaum.
When he heard that Jesus was coming to the regions near him,
he went out to him at Cana, where he knew him to be,
and begged him to come down and heal the son whom he loved.
He believed Christ could heal his son,
requiring his presence, he thought Jesus could heal only by bodily presence and touch,
for he did not believe him to be present everywhere.
Not as the Centurion, who said:
Lord, I am not worthy that you should enter under my roof;
but only say the word, and my servant shall be healed.
consider: The Lord, knowing the heart of the doubter, rebuked the man lukewarm in faith.
He did not reproach him for asking his son's life, but for the defect of his faith.
Yet because out of devotion he pressed his plea, he was heard by the Lord.
Remaining there, Jesus by command and word alone, being absent, healed the son,
to show that he is nowhere lacking, but present everywhere as God.
Go, he said — not willing to go bodily, that he might strip from the man his false notion.
Your son lives: restored to perfect health, from the gate of death he had drawn near.
The man believed the word of Jesus, and went, believing his power was everywhere to heal.
To the proud official the Lord refused to go; to the humble Centurion he offered to come.
Our pride is beaten back, we who venerate in men not the nature made to God's image,
but their honors and their riches.
Weighing what is around them, we fail to see what is within.
Many, neglecting what they are, anxiously seek glory for themselves from elsewhere:
from costly garments, from horses and chariots, from the size of their houses,
from the beauty of columns and the paintings on their walls, they think themselves better men.
Because they have lost what they were as men,
they wander here and there, gathering glory full of every mockery.
Let us not weigh what is around men, but what they are.
There are degrees in faith, as in the other virtues: beginning, increase, and perfection.
This man's faith had its beginning when he begged the Lord to come down;
it received increase when he believed the word, Go, your son lives;
it gained perfection when the servants announced the healing of his son.
Then he himself believed perfectly, and his whole house.
Now this official becomes an evangelist, drawing others into the faith he had received.
prayer: who from Judea of angelic confession and praise came into Galilee, the wheel of this world:
for I am sick, tempted by the beauty of temporal things,
and so I begin to die, worn out by temptation.
Have mercy on me before I die the death of guilt, or of the body, or of hell,
that by the power of your word, by the humility of confession,
by fasting, by prayer, by mercy, and by example,
through your grace I may be freed from the fever of concupiscence and the sickness of sin,
and may yield to no disordered passion. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Jesus tamen ibidem remanens, solo jussu et verbo filium absens sanat, ut pater ejus amplius credat.
latinJewel.english: Yet Jesus, remaining in that very place, by command and word alone, being absent, heals the son — that his father might believe the more.
